behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local scoring logic (token counting + threshold mapping). No module handoff, no real infra, and no LLM dependency. Mocking would not hide the core bug because the bug is inside this function/object.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit tests for the analyzer function with controlled positive/negative lexicons and thresholds. Assert exact sentiment outputs for representative reviews (all-positive, all-negative, empty/no-match).

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about explanation quality/content, which depends on real language generation/interpretation, not just wiring. A mock explainer can trivially satisfy phrase checks and hide real failures (generic output drift, weak grounding). No multi-agent chain required.
behaviors.test_description: Agentic test against the real explanation analyzer/model: submit reviews containing distinct phrases and assert the explanation quotes or closely references those phrases (using strict lexical overlap/grounding criteria) while rejecting generic boilerplate patterns.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in deterministic tie-break logic inside the keyword analyzer. No required cross-module interaction, infra, or model output variability. Integration could catch it too, but is not the minimum boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Unit tests with balanced and near-balanced keyword counts under different tie-break configurations (e.g., prefer negative, prefer neutral). Assert deterministic sentiment outcomes exactly match configured rule.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: Sarcasm interpretation is semantic/model-quality dependent. Deterministic mocks or template stubs would likely pass and miss real intent-detection failures. This is a single model-dependent behavior, not a multi-agent workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Agentic evaluation using real analyzer on a sarcasm-focused dataset. Assert explanations indicate negative intent and cite contradiction cues (positive surface phrase + negative context). Run multiple times to detect instability/regressions.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: Requires module integration (score + explanation assembly) plus real explanation behavior to catch true consistency failures. If explanation is mocked, sentiment-direction agreement can appear correct while real model output disagrees. Only one agentic step is needed, so not Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: End-to-end service test with real explanation analyzer enabled: submit reviews, assert response contains both fields in one payload, and validate explanation sentiment direction aligns with computed score (positive/negative/neutral consistency rules).
